acceptance_targets: [ch03_f02] The revised chapter contains no more than 2 instances of 'the kind of [noun] that [clause]' and no more than 3 instances of 'the way [subject] [verb]' as simile opener. No two instances of either pattern appear within 30 lines of each other. Verify by checking the cited lines — at least 3 of the 5 'the kind of' locations and at least 5 of the 8 'the way' locations must use a different construction.

[ch03_f03] The revised chapter contains no more than 5 'not X but Y,' 'not because X but because Y,' or 'not A, not B, but C' constructions. Each remaining instance must earn its negation — the eliminated alternative must be one the reader would plausibly assume without prompting, not a straw interpretation set up to be knocked down. Verify by checking the 9 cited lines — at least 4 must use a different construction (direct statement, metaphor, physical rendering, or unqualified assertion).

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter contains no more than 2 instances of 'the kind of [noun] that [clause]' and no more than 3 instances of 'the way [subject] [verb]' as simile opener. No two instances of either pattern appear within 30 lines of each other. Verify by checking the cited lines — at least 3 of the 5 'the kind of' locations and at least 5 of the 8 'the way' locations must use a different construction.
findings.evidence: 'the kind of [noun] that [clause]' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:27 ('the kind of observation that sounded casual'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:85 ('the kind of clock that schools and hospitals hung'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:88 ('the kind of mark that came from something concrete'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:153 ('the kind of savings that added up'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:225 ('the kind of laugh that sits at the exact intersection'). Five instances. 'the way [subject] [verb]' simile at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:71, :107, :175, :185, :235, :256, :263, :351. Eight instances.
findings.problem: Two secondary formula patterns recur well past the 2-occurrence threshold. 'The kind of [noun] that [clause]' (5 instances) taxonomizes experience — it sorts feelings and objects into classes rather than rendering them. 'The way [subject] [verb]' (8 instances) is the chapter's default simile structure, appearing every 44 lines on average. Together these constructions account for 13 phrase-level repetitions that, combined with the 7 instances flagged in ch03_f01, create a chapter where roughly 1 in 18 lines deploys a recognizable formula template. The varied-rhythm directive in the style bible ('if three consecutive paragraphs use the same sentence structure, one of them is wrong') is violated not at the paragraph level but at the phrase level — the sentence structures vary, but the embedded phrase shapes do not.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'the kind of' to 2 instances max and 'the way' similes to 3 instances max. For 'the kind of' — retain line 225 (the laugh-at-the-intersection-of-absurdity-and-injury earns its taxonomic framing because Dan is a man whose mind protects itself by classifying). Cut the others. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:27, integrate the observation's impact into Dan's physical response rather than classifying the observation itself. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:85, render the clock directly (cheap, round, ticking its jerky tick) without the institutional-nostalgia classification. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:88, the scar can be rendered as physical detail without the 'kind of mark' frame. For 'the way' similes — retain line 235 (disease-naming) and line 256 (stuck-jar) as the chapter's two strongest. Vary the remaining simile structures: use 'like' comparisons, direct metaphors, or let the described action carry its own weight without a comparative frame. Target lines for revision: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:71, :107, :175, :185, :263, :351.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter contains no more than 5 'not X but Y,' 'not because X but because Y,' or 'not A, not B, but C' constructions. Each remaining instance must earn its negation — the eliminated alternative must be one the reader would plausibly assume without prompting, not a straw interpretation set up to be knocked down. Verify by checking the 9 cited lines — at least 4 must use a different construction (direct statement, metaphor, physical rendering, or unqualified assertion).
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:37 ('not sad about it, just reporting'), :145 ('This was not composure — composure implied effort'), :225 ('not a humorous laugh but the kind of laugh'), :233 ('not a confrontation, not a negotiation, not even a conversation'), :243 ('it wasn't acceptance, it was capitulation dressed in reasonable clothes'), :289 ('Not because the emotion was too large, but because the sentence had too many targets'), :301 ('not mockery but something worse'), :343 ('none of this was a joke, all of it was a joke'), :345 ('not the angry silence of the past months or the exhausted silence of the past weeks but something new'). Nine instances.
findings.problem: The 'not X but Y' negation-then-specification construction appears 9 times. This rhetorical move is effective when used sparingly — it creates precision through elimination, telling the reader what an experience is not before naming what it is. At this density, it becomes the chapter's default analytical posture. Every emotional state, every silence, every ambiguous moment gets the negation treatment. The cumulative effect is a narrator who can only render experience by first saying what it is not, producing a systematically qualifying, hedging prose voice that contradicts the style bible's directive for directness and compression in moments of clarity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce to 4-5 instances max. Retain the strongest uses where negation earns its place: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:145 (Lena's composure — the negation is structurally important because it distinguishes her blankness from effort), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:289 (Mira's faltering — the negation captures her precise analytical habit even as she's losing composure), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:345 (the shared silence — the three-part elimination earns the 'something new' because the reader needs to feel the other silences being ruled out). Cut or revise the others. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:37, 'just reporting' is stronger alone without the throat-clearing 'not sad about it.' At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:225, render the laugh directly — its quality should be legible from the context (absurdity meeting injury in a beige room) without the 'not a humorous laugh but' preamble. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:233, the triple negation ('not a confrontation, not a negotiation, not even a conversation') can be compressed into a single direct statement about what the meeting was. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:301, state what Dan's voice contained rather than what it didn't.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's risk zone is the gap between dignity and absurdity. The characters are adults with professional histories, a child, a mortgage — people with legitimate self-respect — who have been reduced to warm bodies in an optimization framework they cannot understand. The comedy must not protect them from the humiliation; the humiliation must not neutralize the comedy. Render both registers at full force, often in the same scene. The temptation will be to soften the comedy into gentle satire or to darken the humiliation into dystopian grimness — resist both. The novel's territory is the painful and the funny coexisting without one defusing the other. Commit to the specific physical embarrassment of being professionally irrelevant. Commit to the specific tenderness of parenting under surveillance. Commit to the specific grief of a marriage that might still be alive inside a system that has classified it as infrastructure. When in doubt, choose the version that makes you wince and then laugh, or laugh and then wince — both are correct.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to a plot hole. When a scene requires the reader to sit with discomfort — economic shame, marital cruelty, parental failure, institutional humiliation, the specific indignity of being professionally redundant — render it with full specificity. 'They struggled with the situation' is not a sentence that belongs in this novel. Name the thing. Show the body. Let the reader be uncomfortable.
aesthetic_risk_policy.intimacy_and_desire_policy: Dan and Mira's residual physical attraction is a narrative force, not background radiation. When the novel's relational pressure calls for it, render desire with the same specificity as any other emotional state — where in the body it lives, how it changes the air in a room, the precise quality of proximity when two people who know each other's bodies are trying to pretend they don't. The almost-kiss in Chapter 7 should be as fully rendered as any confrontation scene.
aesthetic_risk_policy.register_commitment: This is a dark comedy, not a cautionary tale, not a dystopia, not a satire with a thesis. The novel should not argue about AI. It should render what it feels like to be a body in a world where bodies are overhead. The comic register is not a veneer over a serious message — it IS the message. A scene that is genuinely funny about genuine suffering is doing more literary work than a scene that is solemnly observant about the same material.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all spoken English in this novel. Uncontracted forms should be reserved for deliberate emphasis, Lena Morse's institutional register, or moments where a character is being carefully precise. If a line of dialogue uses 'do not' instead of 'don't' or 'I am' instead of 'I'm,' there should be a reason audible in the scene's pressure.
dialogue_rules.interruption_and_overlap_policy: Use interruptions, sentence fragments, and crosstalk when scene pressure warrants. Dan and Mira in particular should interrupt each other — they know each other's arguments before the sentences end. But every interruption must be motivated; do not scatter dashes for rhythmic texture alone.
dialogue_rules.leverage_shift_guidance: Every exchange of more than three lines should shift the power balance between speakers. This doesn't require overt conflict — a silence, a deflection, a piece of information withheld or accidentally revealed, a joke that lands wrong, a question left unanswered can all shift leverage. The dialogue must be going somewhere, not circling.
dialogue_rules.silence_as_dialogue: Silence, non-answers, and topic changes are valid dialogue moves and often more revealing than speech. When a character doesn't respond to something, the reader should feel the weight of what wasn't said. Render these moments through physical action or observed stillness, not by narrating that the character chose not to speak.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (single-scene compression vs. multi-scene chapters), dialogue density (some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are nearly silent), interiority depth (some chapters live mostly in the POV character's head, others are externally rendered), pacing (some chapters cover hours, others cover weeks in montage), and tonal register (comic chapters adjacent to devastating ones). The contrast between chapters is itself a narrative tool — use it to create whiplash, irony, and accumulating emotional force.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, precise, and register-sensitive. The prose absorbs the vocabulary of its setting: corporate language, tech terminology, bureaucratic formulas — but always filtered through the POV character's relationship to that language. Dan uses corporate words reflexively and with slight discomfort. Mira uses strategic vocabulary as native speech. The narrator should not editorialize on the language; let the words sit in context and let the reader hear the dissonance. Avoid: prestige-literary abstractions that haven't been earned by sensory grounding, and journalistic explainer-prose that breaks character to tell the reader about the world.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating between Dan and Mira as primary POV characters. The camera stays tight to the current POV character's perceptions and vocabulary — what Dan notices is different from what Mira notices. In shared-POV chapters, the rotation should be clear (section breaks or sustained passages in one POV before shifting) rather than head-hopping within a scene.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Short declarative sentences for comic timing, for shock, and for moments of clarity. Longer, more syntactically complex sentences for interiority, sensory immersion, and emotional complexity. The rhythm should accelerate during confrontation and slow during reflection. Avoid settling into any single rhythmic default — if three consecutive paragraphs use the same sentence structure, one of them is wrong.
